reasoning: The informal statement says $(a_1 \cdots a_n)^{-1} = a_n^{-1} \cdots a_1^{-1}$ in a group $G$. The Lean statement quantifies over any group $G$ and any list `as : List G`, with LHS `as.prod⁻¹` = $(a_1 \cdots a_n)^{-1}$ and RHS `(as.reverse.map (λ x => x⁻¹)).prod` = $a_n^{-1} a_{n-1}^{-1} \cdots a_1^{-1}$ (since reversing then mapping inversion produces exactly that list). This is precisely Mathlib's `List.prod_inv_reverse` (up to the standard identity `(L.map f).reverse = L.reverse.map f`). The empty-list case is trivially true and doesn't alter the claim. No extra hypotheses are added and the quantifier structure matches.
